events.title: Big Bang
events.description: The beginning of the universe

links.note: The CMB is the Big Bang’s relic radiation, released ~380,000 years after it

events.title: Cosmic Microwave Background
events.description: The universe becomes transparent; the oldest light we can observe is released.

events.title: First Stars Ignite
events.description: The cosmic dark ages end as the first stars begin to shine.

events.title: First Galaxies Form
events.description: Gravity draws the first stars into galaxies.

events.title: Milky Way Forms
events.description: Our home galaxy begins to take shape.

events.title: Formation of the Solar System
events.description: The Sun and planets form from a collapsing nebula

events.title: Moon-Forming Impact
events.description: A Mars-sized body strikes the young Earth, forming the Moon.

events.title: Formation of Earth
events.description: Earth forms as a rocky planet

events.title: Late Heavy Bombardment
events.description: A period of intense asteroid impacts across the inner Solar System.

events.title: First Life on Earth
events.description: Earliest evidence of single-celled organisms

events.title: Great Oxygenation Event
events.description: Photosynthetic microbes flood the atmosphere with oxygen.

events.title: First Eukaryotic Cells
events.description: Complex cells with nuclei arise.

events.title: Snowball Earth
events.description: Global glaciation nearly freezes the entire planet.

events.title: Cambrian Explosion
events.description: Most major animal groups appear in a burst of evolution.

events.title: First Land Plants
events.description: Plants colonize dry land for the first time.

events.title: First Vertebrates on Land
events.description: Fish-like tetrapods crawl onto land.

events.title: Permian–Triassic Extinction
events.description: The "Great Dying" wipes out ~90% of species — the worst mass extinction.

links.note: The “Great Dying” emptied ecosystems; dinosaurs rose in the aftermath

events.title: First Dinosaurs
events.description: Dinosaurs emerge and begin their long reign.

events.title: First Mammals
events.description: Small mammals appear alongside the early dinosaurs.

events.title: First Birds
events.description: Feathered dinosaurs such as Archaeopteryx take to the air.

events.title: First Flowering Plants
events.description: Angiosperms evolve and reshape the biosphere.

events.title: Dinosaur Extinction
events.description: Mass extinction event, likely caused by asteroid impact

links.note: The extinction cleared the niches into which mammals — and primates — radiated

events.title: First Primates
events.description: The primate lineage that leads to humans begins.

events.title: Hominins Split from Chimpanzees
events.description: The human and chimpanzee lineages diverge.

events.title: Lucy (Australopithecus)
events.description: Early bipedal hominins walk the African savanna.

events.title: Homo erectus
events.description: An upright human ancestor spreads out of Africa and uses tools.

events.title: Discovery of Fire
events.description: Controlled use of fire by early humans.

events.title: Homo sapiens Emerges
events.description: Modern humans appear in Africa

events.title: Out of Africa Migration
events.description: Modern humans begin spreading across the globe.

events.title: Cave Paintings of Chauvet
events.description: Some of the earliest known figurative art is painted.

events.title: First Settlements in America
events.description: Humans cross Bering land bridge into America.

events.title: End of the Last Ice Age
events.description: The Holocene begins as glaciers retreat, enabling agriculture.

links.note: A warm, stable Holocene climate made farming viable

events.title: Agricultural Revolution
events.description: Humans begin farming and settling

links.note: Farming surpluses fed the first permanent cities

events.title: Göbekli Tepe
events.description: Hunter-gatherers build the earliest known monumental temple.

events.title: Founding of Jericho
events.description: One of the earliest continuously inhabited settlements.

events.title: Sumer and the First Cities
events.description: Urban civilization emerges in Mesopotamia.

events.title: Discovery of the Wheel
events.description: Earliest known use of the wheel in Mesopotamia.

events.title: Discovery of Bronze
events.description: Start of the Bronze Age, use of bronze for tools and weapons.

events.title: Indus Valley Civilization
events.description: A sophisticated urban culture flourishes in the Indus basin.

events.title: Invention of Writing (Cuneiform)
events.description: Sumerians record language in clay — the dawn of recorded history.

links.note: From pictographic cuneiform toward the alphabet most modern scripts descend from

events.title: Ancient Egypt
events.description: Egyptian civilization begins

events.title: Construction of Stonehenge
events.description: Neolithic Britons raise the iconic stone circle over centuries.

events.title: Great Pyramid of Giza
events.description: The largest Egyptian pyramid is built as a pharaoh’s tomb.

events.title: Akkadian Empire
events.description: Sargon of Akkad forges the first known empire.

events.title: Code of Hammurabi
events.description: Babylon issues one of the earliest written legal codes.

events.title: Discovery of Steel
events.description: Early production of steel in Anatolia.

events.title: Bronze Age Collapse
events.description: Mediterranean civilizations collapse in a wave of upheaval.

events.title: Phoenician Alphabet
events.description: A phonetic alphabet spreads across the Mediterranean, ancestor of many scripts.

events.title: First Olympic Games
events.description: Greek city-states gather for athletic games at Olympia.

events.title: Homer’s Iliad and Odyssey
events.description: The foundational epics of Greek literature are composed.

events.title: Discovery of Electricity
events.description: Ancient Greeks observe static electricity.

events.title: Life of the Buddha
events.description: Siddhartha Gautama founds Buddhism in northern India.

events.title: Life of Confucius
events.description: The philosopher shapes East Asian thought for millennia.

events.title: Achaemenid Persian Empire
events.description: Cyrus the Great founds the first great Persian empire.

events.title: Roman Republic Founded
events.description: Rome overthrows its kings and establishes a republic.

events.title: Athenian Democracy
events.description: Athens pioneers direct democracy under Cleisthenes.

events.title: Life of Socrates
events.description: The Socratic method reshapes Western philosophy.

events.title: Alexander the Great’s Empire
events.description: Alexander conquers from Greece to India, spreading Hellenistic culture.

links.note: Founded in Alexander’s namesake city under his successor dynasty

events.title: Maurya Empire
events.description: The first empire to unify most of the Indian subcontinent.

events.title: Euclid’s Elements
events.description: Euclid systematizes geometry in the most influential textbook in history.

events.title: Library of Alexandria
events.description: The ancient world’s greatest center of learning is founded.

events.title: Punic Wars
events.description: Rome defeats Carthage to dominate the Mediterranean.

links.note: Victory over Carthage left Rome master of the Mediterranean

events.title: Archimedes of Syracuse
events.description: Foundational work in mathematics, mechanics, and hydrostatics.

events.title: Qin Unification of China
events.description: Qin Shi Huang unifies China and begins the Great Wall.

events.title: Han Dynasty
events.description: A golden age of Chinese civilization and the Silk Road.

events.title: Assassination of Julius Caesar
events.description: Caesar’s death hastens the end of the Roman Republic.

links.note: The civil wars after Caesar’s murder ended the Republic; Octavian became Augustus

events.title: Roman Empire
events.description: From Augustus becoming the first emperor to the fall of the Western Roman Empire.

events.title: Birth of Jesus and Christianity
events.description: The founding figure of Christianity, the world’s largest religion.

events.title: Invention of Paper
events.description: Cai Lun refines papermaking in Han China.

links.note: Movable type needed cheap paper, which reached Europe via the Islamic world

events.title: Gupta Empire
events.description: A golden age of Indian science, mathematics, and art.

events.title: Council of Nicaea
events.description: The early Christian church defines core doctrine.

events.title: Founding of Islam
events.description: Muhammad begins preaching Islam in Arabia.

events.title: Tang Dynasty
events.description: A cosmopolitan high point of Chinese culture.

events.title: Islamic Golden Age
events.description: Flourishing of science, mathematics, and medicine across the Muslim world.

events.title: Charlemagne Crowned Emperor
events.description: Charlemagne is crowned, reviving imperial rule in Western Europe.

events.title: Al-Khwarizmi and Algebra
events.description: The foundational text of algebra, giving the field its name.

events.title: Invention of Gunpowder
events.description: Chinese alchemists discover gunpowder.

links.note: Ottoman cannon breached walls that had withstood sieges for a thousand years

events.title: Leif Erikson Reaches America
events.description: Norse explorers reach North America, centuries before Columbus.

events.title: Magnetic Compass Navigation
events.description: The compass is adapted for navigation in Song China.

events.title: Norman Conquest of England
events.description: William the Conqueror reshapes English history at Hastings.

events.title: Mongol Empire
events.description: Genghis Khan founds the largest contiguous land empire in history.

events.title: Magna Carta
events.description: English barons limit the king’s power, seeding constitutional rule.

events.title: The Black Death
events.description: Plague kills a third of Europe’s population.

events.title: The Renaissance
events.description: A rebirth of art, science, and learning in Europe.

events.title: Inca Empire
events.description: The largest empire in pre-Columbian America.

events.title: Printing Press Invented
events.description: Gutenberg invents the movable type printing press

links.note: Cheap printed pamphlets spread Luther’s ideas faster than any censor

events.title: Fall of Constantinople
events.description: The Ottomans capture the city, ending the Byzantine Empire.

links.note: Greek scholars fleeing west carried classical texts into Renaissance Italy
Ottoman control of eastern trade routes pushed Europe to seek sea routes west

events.title: Discovery of America by Columbus
events.description: Christopher Columbus lands in the Americas.

links.note: Columbus reached the Americas; Magellan’s expedition proved the globe could be sailed

events.title: Protestant Reformation
events.description: Luther’s 95 Theses split Western Christianity.

events.title: Magellan’s Circumnavigation
events.description: The first voyage around the world.

events.title: Copernicus: Heliocentrism
events.description: Copernicus places the Sun, not the Earth, at the center.

links.note: Kepler replaced Copernicus’ circles with ellipses that actually fit the data

events.title: Shakespeare’s Plays
events.description: Shakespeare writes the most influential works in English literature.

events.title: Kepler’s Laws of Planetary Motion
events.description: Kepler shows the planets move in ellipses.

links.note: Newton’s gravitation derived Kepler’s laws from first principles

events.title: Galileo’s Telescopic Discoveries
events.description: Galileo’s observations confirm the Copernican model.

links.note: Galileo’s mechanics laid the groundwork Newton unified

events.title: Harvey Describes Blood Circulation
events.description: William Harvey explains how blood circulates through the body.

events.title: The Enlightenment
events.description: Reason, liberty, and science reshape European thought.

events.title: Newton's Principia
events.description: Isaac Newton publishes Philosophiæ Naturalis Principia Mathematica

links.note: Einstein’s curved spacetime superseded Newtonian gravity at extreme mass and speed

events.title: Linnaean Taxonomy
events.description: Linnaeus introduces the modern system for naming species.

events.title: Industrial Revolution
events.description: Start of the Industrial Revolution in Britain.

events.title: Watt’s Steam Engine
events.description: James Watt’s efficient engine powers the Industrial Revolution.

links.note: Efficient steam power was the revolution’s prime mover

events.title: Independence of USA
events.description: Declaration of Independence signed.

links.note: The American example — and the war debt France took on aiding it — fed revolution

events.title: French Revolution
events.description: Start of the French Revolution.

links.note: Revolutionary France’s wars flowed into Napoleon’s empire-building

events.title: Lavoisier Founds Modern Chemistry
events.description: Lavoisier establishes chemistry as a quantitative science.

events.title: Jenner’s Smallpox Vaccine
events.description: The first vaccine launches the field of immunization.

events.title: Volta’s Electric Battery
events.description: Volta builds the first battery, a steady source of current.

events.title: Napoleonic Wars
events.description: Napoleon’s wars reshape Europe.

events.title: Dalton’s Atomic Theory
events.description: Dalton revives the atom as the basis of matter.

events.title: First Camera
events.description: Joseph Nicéphore Niépce creates the first camera.

events.title: Faraday’s Electromagnetic Induction
events.description: Faraday discovers how magnetism generates electricity.

links.note: Maxwell turned Faraday’s field intuitions into equations

events.title: Telegraph
events.description: Morse’s telegraph enables near-instant long-distance messaging.

events.title: Theory of Evolution
events.description: Charles Darwin publishes On the Origin of Species

links.note: Mendel’s heredity later supplied the mechanism Darwin’s theory needed

events.title: First Combustion Engine
events.description: Étienne Lenoir builds first practical internal combustion engine.

events.title: American Civil War
events.description: The Union defeats the Confederacy; slavery is abolished in the US.

events.title: Pasteur’s Germ Theory
events.description: Pasteur shows that microorganisms cause disease.

events.title: Maxwell’s Equations
events.description: Maxwell unifies electricity, magnetism, and light.

links.note: Radio waves existed in Maxwell’s equations before anyone transmitted them

events.title: Mendel’s Laws of Heredity
events.description: Mendel discovers the basic rules of inheritance.

links.note: Mendel’s abstract “factors” found their molecule a century later

events.title: Mendeleev’s Periodic Table
events.description: Mendeleev organizes the elements and predicts undiscovered ones.

events.title: Telephone
events.description: Alexander Graham Bell patents the telephone.

events.title: First Light Bulb
events.description: Thomas Edison invents practical incandescent light bulb.

events.title: First Car
events.description: Karl Benz builds first automobile.

events.title: Radio
events.description: Marconi demonstrates wireless telegraphy.

events.title: X-Rays Discovered
events.description: Röntgen discovers X-rays, revolutionizing medicine.

events.title: Radioactivity Discovered
events.description: Marie and Pierre Curie pioneer the study of radioactivity.

events.title: First Commercial Camera
events.description: Kodak releases the Brownie camera.

events.title: Planck’s Quantum Theory
events.description: Planck introduces energy quanta, founding quantum physics.

links.note: Planck’s reluctant quanta of 1900 seeded the full theory of 1925

events.title: First Commercial Electric Washing Machine
events.description: Alva J. Fisher invents electric washing machine.

events.title: First Plane
events.description: Wright brothers' first powered flight.

events.title: Theory of Relativity
events.description: Einstein publishes special relativity

links.note: Extending special relativity to gravity took Einstein another decade

events.title: Rutherford’s Atomic Nucleus
events.description: Rutherford discovers the dense nucleus at the atom’s core.

events.title: First Commercial Electric Refrigerator
events.description: Fred W. Wolf invents first domestic electric refrigerator.

events.title: World War I
events.description: Global war centered in Europe.

links.note: Versailles’ unresolved grievances and the interwar crisis set the stage

events.title: First Commercial Plane
events.description: First scheduled commercial airline flight.

events.title: General Relativity
events.description: Einstein reframes gravity as the curvature of spacetime.

events.title: Russian Revolution
events.description: The Bolsheviks establish the world’s first communist state.

events.title: Quantum Mechanics
events.description: Heisenberg and Schrödinger formalize the quantum world.

events.title: Discovery of Penicillin
events.description: Fleming’s discovery launches the age of antibiotics.

events.title: First Commercial Electric Dishwasher
events.description: First electric dishwasher by Miele.

events.title: Hubble: The Expanding Universe
events.description: Hubble shows the universe is expanding, implying the Big Bang.

links.note: The observed expansion, run backwards in time, pointed to a hot, dense origin

events.title: The Great Depression
events.description: A global economic collapse reshapes politics and society.

events.title: First Helicopter
events.description: Focke-Wulf Fw 61, first practical helicopter.

events.title: Nuclear Fission Discovered
events.description: Splitting the atom opens the path to nuclear power and weapons.

links.note: From laboratory fission to the Trinity test in seven years

events.title: World War II
events.description: Global war involving the vast majority of the world's nations.

links.note: Founded by the victors to prevent a third world war
The wartime allies’ rivalry hardened into a bipolar world

events.title: The Holocaust
events.description: Nazi Germany systematically murders six million Jews.

links.note: The genocide was perpetrated under cover of the wider war

events.title: First Nuke Detonated
events.description: Trinity test, first nuclear explosion.

events.title: United Nations Founded
events.description: Nations form the UN to prevent future world wars.

events.title: First Commercial Helicopter
events.description: Sikorsky S-51, first commercial helicopter.

events.title: The Transistor
events.description: Bell Labs invents the building block of all modern electronics.

links.note: Packing many transistors onto one chip

events.title: Indian Independence
events.description: India wins independence from British rule.

events.title: Cold War
events.description: Decades of tension between the US and Soviet blocs.

events.title: Founding of BRD (West Germany)
events.description: Federal Republic of Germany founded.

events.title: Founding of DDR (East Germany)
events.description: German Democratic Republic founded.

events.title: DNA Double Helix
events.description: Watson, Crick, and Franklin reveal the structure of DNA.

links.note: Knowing DNA’s structure opened the path to reading entire genomes

events.title: First Computer (Mainframe)
events.description: IBM 305 RAMAC, first commercial computer with hard disk.

events.title: First Orbiting Satellite (Sputnik 1)
events.description: Soviet Union launches Sputnik 1, first artificial satellite.

links.note: Sputnik ignited the space race the Moon landing won

events.title: Integrated Circuit
events.description: The microchip packs many transistors onto one wafer.

links.note: Microprocessors made computers personal

events.title: Berlin Wall
events.description: The wall divides Berlin from its construction in 1961 until its fall in 1989.

links.note: The Wall was the Cold War’s most concrete symbol

events.title: First Human in Space
events.description: Yuri Gagarin becomes first human in space.

events.title: First LED
events.description: Nick Holonyak invents the first visible-spectrum LED.

events.title: Cuban Missile Crisis
events.description: The world comes closest to nuclear war.

links.note: The closest the Cold War came to turning hot

events.title: US Civil Rights Act
events.description: Landmark law outlaws discrimination in the United States.

events.title: Plate Tectonics Confirmed
events.description: Continental drift is unified into the theory of plate tectonics.

events.title: Moon Landing
events.description: Apollo 11 lands humans on the Moon

events.title: Creation of Internet (ARPANET)
events.description: ARPANET, precursor to the Internet, goes live.

links.note: The Web runs atop the network ARPANET grew into

events.title: First Personal Computer
events.description: Kenbak-1, considered the first personal computer.

events.title: First Commercial Electronic Camera
events.description: First digital camera prototype by Kodak.

events.title: World Wide Web
events.description: Tim Berners-Lee invents the World Wide Web

events.title: Creation of Linux
events.description: Linus Torvalds releases first version of Linux.

events.title: Fall of the Soviet Union
events.description: The USSR dissolves, ending the Cold War.

events.title: First Blue LED
events.description: Shuji Nakamura creates the first blue LED.

links.note: Milestone in artificial lighting, like the incandescent bulb.

events.title: GPS Goes Operational
events.description: The Global Positioning System reaches full operation.

events.title: First RGB LED
events.description: First commercial RGB LED released.

events.title: Creation of ISS (First Humans Arrive)
events.description: First crew arrives at the International Space Station.

events.title: September 11 Attacks
events.description: Coordinated attacks on the US reshape 21st-century geopolitics.

events.title: Human Genome Project
events.description: Scientists sequence the complete human genome.

events.title: Social Media Era
events.description: Platforms like Facebook reshape how billions connect.

events.title: Creation of Git
events.description: Linus Torvalds creates Git version control system.

events.title: First iPhone
events.description: Apple releases the first iPhone.

events.title: Higgs Boson Discovered
events.description: The LHC confirms the particle that gives mass to matter.

events.title: CRISPR Gene Editing
events.description: A precise tool for editing DNA transforms biology.

events.title: Gravitational Waves Detected
events.description: LIGO detects ripples in spacetime from colliding black holes.

events.title: Reusable Rockets
events.description: SpaceX lands an orbital-class rocket booster for reuse.

events.title: First Image of a Black Hole
events.description: The Event Horizon Telescope pictures a black hole for the first time.

events.title: COVID-19 Pandemic
events.description: A global pandemic reshapes daily life and accelerates mRNA vaccines.

events.title: James Webb Space Telescope
events.description: JWST begins peering into the early universe in infrared.

events.title: Large Language Models
events.description: Conversational AI systems reach mainstream use.

links.note: Large language models kicked off the current AI wave

events.title: AI Renaissance
events.description: Advanced AI models become mainstream

events.title: Climate Stabilization (Speculative)
events.description: Humanity successfully stabilizes climate

events.title: Earth’s Oceans Evaporate
events.description: A brightening Sun renders Earth uninhabitable.

links.note: Two stages of the Sun’s slow brightening toward its death

events.title: Andromeda–Milky Way Collision
events.description: Our galaxy merges with Andromeda.

events.title: Sun Becomes Red Giant
events.description: The Sun expands and likely engulfs Earth

links.note: After the red-giant phase, the Sun’s core remains as a white dwarf

events.title: Sun Becomes a White Dwarf
events.description: The Sun sheds its layers and fades into a white dwarf.
